title: melaleuca ebracteata var. ebracteata
intro: Melaleuca ebracteata var. ebracteata, commonly known as the Quill Leaf Paperbark, is a plant species native to Western Australia. It is a shrub with aromatic leaves and bark, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial actions. The plant's health benefits include treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and combat infections. The plant's leaves can be prepared as a tea or infused in oil to create a topical treatment, and it is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
